summary: Improved Miller soundboard capture delivers a decent listening experience, while the early‑70 set is average overall but shines on Me & My Uncle and Black Peter.
review: The Miller 127228 soundboard source sounds noticeably better than earlier versions, offering clear balance without the speed or image problems of other transfers. Musically the show is an unremarkable January ’70 performance – missing a few opening numbers and feeling a bit rushed – yet it features a standout Me & My Uncle and a solid Black Peter, with the Mason's Children track sounding a little rickety.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is judged average (~2.8/5) while recording quality is good (~4/5); weighted combination yields a mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Miller's updated 127228 transfer sounds way better than older circulating sources; previous Miller and other versions suffered balance, image, and speed/pitch issues. This version is clean but not flawless.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Typical early‑70 set with missing opener(s) and a hurried feel due to venue constraints; lacks the extended jam sequences that make other shows memorable.

band_member_comments.Keys: Brief nod to Pigpen’s presence ('Pigpen. nuff said.')
